original: Largest cosmic explosion ever recorded (AT2021lwx)- fireball 100 x  size of the solar system with a brightness 2trillion x the sun’s, thought to be large gas cloud entering a supermassive black hole eight million lights years away
Fossilized dinosaur eggs with embryos are discovered in China
Challenger astronauts complete 1st in space satellite repair
Russian volcano Shiveluch erupts spewing ash 20 kilometers into the air in the country's eastern Kamchatka region
Dodge Morgan sailed solo nonstop around world in 150 days
Halley's Comet makes closest approach to Earth this trip, 63 M km
Apple I computer, created bySteve Wozniak, is released
1st playoff goal scored by a goalie,Ron Hextallof visiting Philadelphia Flyers shoots into Washington Capitols empty net while his team is shorthanded in 8-5 win
J. P. Parisé scores an 11 sec OT goal, to see the NY Islanders eliminate the NY Rangers in their ever 1st playoff advance
Hungarian swimmer Alfréd Hajós beats Otto Herschmann of Austria by 0.6s to win the inaugural Olympic 100m freestyle final in 1:22.2 at the Athens Games; also takes out the 1,200m on the same day
Ryan X-13 Vertijet becomes 1st jet to take-off & land vertically
NASA launches RCA-F
8.6 magnitude earthquake and 8.2 aftershock occurs off the coast of Indonesia
Israel's Beresheet spacecraft, the first privately-funded mission to the moon, crashes on the moon
Jane Bolin, first African American female judge, born in Poughkeepsie, New York (d. 2007)
Andrew Wiles, British mathematician (number theory, proved Fermat's last theorem), born in Cambridge, England
Piers J Sellers, English astronaut and climate scientist, born in Sussex (d. 2016)
Apollo 13 launched to Moon; unable to land, returns in 6 days
Stanley Cup Final, Maple Leaf Gardens, Toronto, ON: Detroit Red Wings beat Toronto Maple Leafs, 3-2 for a 3-1 series win; Red Wings first SC Championship
The first modern submarine designed and built byJohn Philip Holland, USS Holland is acquired by the U.S. Navy
Wentworth Cheswell, American auditor and teacher, 1st African American elected to public office in the US, born in Newmarket, New Hampshire (d. 1817)
Soyuz T-11 returns to Earth
The 12th century Lewis chess pieces are exhibited at the Society of Antiquaries of Scotland, shortly after their rediscovery in a sand bank on the Scottish Isle of Lewis
Emmett Ashford becomes first African American major league baseball umpire in Washington Senators’ game against the Cleveland Indians at D.C. Stadium
Hostilities formally cease in the American Revolutionary War after the Continental Congress proclaims the "Cessation of Arms" against His Britannic Majesty
Battle of Rivas; Costa Rica beats William Walker's invading Nicaraguans
Ellis Island, New York, designated as an immigration station
40 tornadoes strike US midwest killing 272 & injuring 5,000
Beatles' "Let It Be" single goes #1 and stays #1 for 2 weeks
Zoja Ivanova wins 2nd female World Cup marathon (2:30:39)
NY Lotto pays $35 million to two winner (#s are 6-14-24-32-34-51)
Space Shuttle STS 37 (Atlantis 8) lands at Edwards Air Force Base, California
MLB Cleveland Indians set team record for long game loss to Boston Red Sox (19 innings - 6½ hr)
Updated "Tree of Life" published in "Nature Microbiology" by scientists from Berkeley University in California
First day of voting in Indian general elections, the world's largest democracy with 900 million eligible voters in the world's largest-ever election (till 23rd May)
Tropical cyclone Seroja makes landfall in Western Australia near the town of Kalbarri with winds up to 170km/h (105mph)
NHL Boston Bruins break record for most team points in a season at 133, in a 5-2 victory over visiting Washington Capitols; Montreal Canadiens held record of 132 since 1977
Robert Schumannhears Italian violin virtuoso Niccolò Paganini play in Frankfurt
RMS Titanicleaves Queenstown, Ireland, for NY
"TheAvengers", directed byJoss Whedonand starringRobert Downey Jr.,Chris Evans,Chris Hemsworth,Scarlett Johansson,Mark RuffaloandJeremy Renner, premieres in Los Angeles, California
Barack ObamaandRaúl Castromeet in Panama, the first meeting of US and Cuban heads of state since the Cuban Revolution
Norman McLaren, Scottish-Canadian film animator and director, born in Stirling, Scotland (d. 1987)

Dorothy Schroeder, American baseball shortstop in the All-American Girls Professional Baseball League (1943-54), pictured in the Women in Baseball exhibit at the National Baseball Hall of Fame and Museum, New York, born in Sadorus, Illinois (d. 1996)
Denis Goldberg, South African anti-apartheid activist (convicted alongside Mandela), born in Cape Town (d. 2020)
Neville Staple, English ska singer (The Specials - "Ghost Town"; Fun Boy Three), born in Manchester, Jamaica
Brooks Hall in Civic Center dedicated in San Francisco, California
MLB California Angels Mark Langston & Mike Witt, no-hit Seattle, 1-0
Father Abraham [Pierre Kartner], Dutch singer and songwriter (The Smurf Song), born in Elst, Netherlands (d. 2022)
Frederick "Rick" Hauck, American astronaut (STS-7, STS 51-A, STS-26), born in Long Beach, California
Billy Bowden, New Zealand cricket umpire (84 Tests, 200 ODIs, 24 T20Is), born in Auckland, New Zealand
Henry Rawlinson, British army officer and oriental scholar (major role in deciphering cuneiform), born in Chadlington, England (d. 1895)
KingWilliam IIIand Queen Mary II crowned as joint rulers of England, Scotland and Ireland
Great Britain, Spain, the Netherlands, Prussia, Savoy, Portugal & France agree the Treaty of Utrecht; France cedes Maritime provinces in North America to Britain
Jack Slack retains the Champion of England boxing title after defeating Frenchman Jean Petit in 7 rounds in Harlston, England, acknowledged as the first international prizefight
Spelman College founded as Atlanta Baptist Female Seminary in basement of Friendship Baptist Church, Atlanta
Irish tennis player John Boland, representing Great Britain wins both the men's singles and doubles finals at the Athens Olympics; Dionysios Kasdaglis of Greece loses both matches
Abd el-Krims Rifkabylen beats French army in Morocco
American helicopter designer Frank Piasecki, Vertol founder, flies his 1st (single-rotor) craft
Ireland sets fishing zone at 50 mile
Detroit Red Wings become 2nd NHL team to win 60 games in a season
